docs.title: Adult Game Manager
docs.text:
Adult Game Manager is a local-first Android companion for tracking adult game updates across installed APKs, JoiPlay games, and source catalogs.

Download latest APK Report an issue Verify releases

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: Area Summary Installed games Lists installed APKs and imported JoiPlay games in one place. Catalog Searches a game catalog by title, tags, status, engine, rating, and installed state. Matching Links local games to catalog entries and shows version/update state. Install helpers Opens local APKs and JoiPlay archives you select; it does not download games for you. Backup/config Exports/imports app state and supports advanced app_config.json overrides. Diagnostics Saves local logs and, when configured, uploads logs/screenshots only after you tap upload.

docs.title: Start here
docs.text:
• Getting started covers installation and first-run setup.
  • Main screen tour explains the installed-games list.
  • Catalog explains source catalogs, filters, and opening game pages.
  • Matching games explains how installed games connect to catalog entries.
  • JoiPlay covers local JoiPlay imports and install helpers.
  • Diagnostics explains logs, local saves, and optional upload.
  • Trust and verification lists source, checksums, and privacy notes.

docs.title: Privacy basics
docs.text:
• No site login is required.
  • No hosted account, ads, or analytics SDKs.
  • No automatic game downloader.
  • Diagnostics upload is opt-in and only appears when configured.

docs.title: Docs maintenance rules
docs.text: These rules keep the Adult Game Manager help site in sync with the shipping app.

docs.title: Hard rules
docs.text:
1. Any UI change requires a docs change in the same release. If you add, rename, remove, or change the behavior of any menu item, dialog, screen, or row icon, update the matching page under docs/.
  2. Each menu item or feature has exactly one canonical page. Use the navigation map in mkdocs.yml to find it.
  3. Update docs/changelog.md with one line per user-visible change, linking to the affected docs page.
  4. Bump the last_updated: front matter at the top of every page you touch to today's date.
  5. Screenshots live in docs/screenshots/. Reference from top-level pages with screenshots/name.png and from nested pages with ../screenshots/name.png.
  6. GitHub Pages is published by .github/workflows/docs.yml after Pages is enabled for the repository.

docs.title: Page conventions
docs.text:
• Open with a one-sentence summary of what the feature does.
  • Include a "Where to find it" section for menu items.
  • Prefer exact menu names from the app.
  • Keep wording source-neutral unless a feature is specifically about F95Zone or JoiPlay.

docs.title: Feature map
docs.text: Feature Page First-run welcome and F95 Updater migration docs/getting-started.md Installed games list, status colors, search, multi-select docs/main-screen.md Manual matching dialog docs/mapping/manual-search.md Paste page URL docs/mapping/paste-url.md "Not in catalog" flag docs/mapping/not-in-catalog.md Catalog sync and source catalogs docs/catalog/sync.md Refresh installed games from catalog docs/catalog/refresh.md Catalog filters docs/catalog/browse-filter.md Review unmapped games docs/catalog/review-unmapped.md Auto-hide non-games docs/catalog/auto-hide.md JoiPlay install/import/settings/delete docs/joiplay/*.md APK install helper docs/installs/install-apk.md Backup import/export and auto-backups docs/backup/*.md Drop-in app_config.json docs/backup/app-config.md Logs, diagnostics, optional upload, screenshots docs/diagnostics/logs.md App self-update docs/self-update.md

docs.title: Backup and config
docs.text: Backup and config tools preserve your mappings and support advanced release/catalog settings.

docs.title: Where to find it
docs.text: Menu -> Backup & config ...

docs.title: Backups
docs.text:
Export your app state before testing risky changes or moving to another device. Imports restore mapping state and personal tracking data included in the backup.

See Export and import backup for the full workflow.

docs.title: Advanced app config
docs.text:
Advanced users can provide an app_config.json to override catalog, update, support, donation, and optional diagnostics-upload settings.

Adult Game Manager looks for drop-in config files in common Documents/Download locations and logs a safe summary when a config is loaded. It does not print upload tokens in logs.

See App config for supported override types and safety notes.

docs.title: Catalog
docs.text: The Catalog tab lets you search source catalogs even for games that are not currently installed.

docs.title: Filters
docs.text: Use filters for installed state, source, platform, status, engine, rating, and sort mode. Tag search supports typed tag filters.

docs.title: Opening a page
docs.text: Open a catalog entry to view details and launch the related page in your browser. The app does not download game files automatically.

docs.title: Source catalogs
docs.text: Adult Game Manager uses a source-neutral catalog model. A source can be F95Zone, AdultGameWorld, or another configured catalog feed. Catalog entries keep their source name and canonical page URL so source-specific links still open the right site.

docs.title: Related pages
docs.text:
• Sources and sync
  • Refresh installed games
  • Browse and filter
  • Review unmapped games

docs.title: v1.0.62
docs.text:
• Android 10 storage permission fix: AGM now requests legacy external-storage permissions on Android 10 and below instead of assuming All files access exists.
  • Added a persistent installed-library view type toggle in the main menu.
  • Card view now uses the cover-first card layout while opening the same full action popup menu as list view.
  • Card/list preference is saved across app restarts.

docs.title: v1.0.58
docs.text:
• Save editor update.
  • Ren'Py and RPGM save editor filters now include a Whole word option, so searches like 1 do not also match 10 or 11.
  • Ren'Py and RPGM save edits can optionally overwrite a matching same-name save in a nearby sync folder, with a backup made before replacing the sync copy.
  • JoiPlay upgrades now record the source archive/version marker, helping AGM avoid stale folder-name version detection after upgrade-in-place flows.
  • JoiPlay matching uses additional internal metadata when available, including Ren'Py build_info/options and RPGM/HTML titles.

docs.title: v1.0.51
docs.text:
• Trust/transparency release.
  • Public source ZIP is attached to the GitHub release.
  • Added Apache-2.0 project licensing, privacy/permissions notes, third-party notices, checksums, and a GitHub Actions build workflow.
  • Public defaults no longer include developer donation/payment links.
  • Release signing material and private config are excluded from source.

docs.title: v1.0.50
docs.text:
• Open folder now uses Android's native implicit folder-view/default-app flow.
  • The folder action no longer forces a specific file manager package.
  • If no file manager can open folders, AGM shows a message instead of opening the Android "Use this folder" permission picker.

docs.title: v1.0.48
docs.text:
• UI and cleanup release.
  • Tapping a game row now opens the grouped Game actions menu.
  • The top list summary moved behind an info icon popup to keep the title bar cleaner.
  • Main menu labels and save-tool grouping were clarified.
  • Android receiver registration lint was fixed.
  • Launcher monochrome icon metadata was added.
  • Minor stale storage comments/checks were cleaned up.
  • SDK target remains unchanged.

docs.title: v1.0.44
docs.text:
• Public-readiness fixes from code audit.
  • RPGM save inspection is capped for large saves and shows a warning instead of trying to flatten unlimited values.
  • RPGM save writes now use temp-file verification before replacing the live save.
  • RPGM backups can be restored in-app.
  • Support links ignore legacy F95 Updater thread overrides.
  • JoiPlay summaries and sorting use scanned total sizes consistently.
  • Android-only size sorts are hidden unless the Android source filter is active.
  • Backup export is renamed for Adult Game Manager.
  • Ren'Py large integer edits are supported.

docs.title: v1.0.42
docs.text:
• Fixed an RPGM save-edit crash/OOM on large saves.
  • RPGM write verification now reads only the edited value path instead of flattening the full save tree.
  • Backup, re-encode, re-read, and value verification safety checks remain in place.

docs.title: v1.0.39
docs.text:
• Improved landscape dialogs across Adult Game Manager.
  • Save editors, compare views, file/folder pickers, save-location reports, association pickers, JoiPlay settings/browser, and catalog details now use side-by-side layouts where appropriate.
  • Fixed the in-app support link so About/Share opens the public F95Zone thread.
  • This release is especially useful on tablets, foldables, Samsung DeX, and phones used in landscape orientation.

docs.title: v1.0
docs.text:
• Public launch release of Adult Game Manager.
  • In-app Documentation opens the live Adult Game Manager help site.
  • Public GitHub update feed and latest APK assets are live.
  • Multi-source catalog support is enabled for F95Zone and AdultGameWorld.
  • The public F95Zone thread is now the main support/discussion thread.

docs.title: v0.2.13
docs.text:
• In-app Documentation now opens the working GitHub help landing page while GitHub Pages is pending repository enablement.
  • Expanded Adult Game Manager help from a starter README into a full MkDocs help tree covering permissions, matching, catalog sync, installs, backups, diagnostics, self-update, support, and FAQ.

docs.title: v0.2.11
docs.text:
• Public help site created for Adult Game Manager.
  • Help screenshots were refreshed from the in-app walkthrough capture.
  • Diagnostics help now documents local save, optional upload, and screenshot capture behavior.

docs.title: v0.2.x
docs.text:
• Adult Game Manager moved app/catalog/update assets to the public adult-game-manager-releases repository.
  • Runtime config supports catalog URLs, multiple update feeds, support/issues/donation links, and optional diagnostics upload.

docs.title: Diagnostics
docs.text: Diagnostics tools help troubleshoot app behavior without guessing.

docs.title: Where to find it
docs.text:
Menu -> Help ...

Always available:

  • Copy diagnostics summary - opens a summary you can copy into an issue.
  • Save local diagnostics - writes logs and queued crash reports to your Documents folder.

When diagnostics are enabled in app_config.json, more actions may appear:

  • Upload crash logs - uploads queued crash reports to the configured upload target.
  • Upload app logs + screenshots - uploads current app logs plus captured walkthrough screenshots.
  • Capture launch screenshots - captures launch/onboarding screens for help docs.
  • Capture walkthrough screenshots - captures the main help-site walkthrough screens.
  • Verbose logs - toggles extra logging for the current run.

docs.title: Privacy
docs.text:
Diagnostics upload is explicit. The app does not upload logs or screenshots automatically, and the upload menu only appears when upload settings are configured.

The canonical diagnostics page is now Diagnostics and logs.

docs.title: Troubleshooting / FAQ
docs.text: Common Adult Game Manager problems usually come from stale catalogs, Android file permissions, or old private config.

docs.title: The app opens GitHub README instead of a polished help site
docs.text: That is expected until GitHub Pages is enabled for this repository. The full MkDocs help source is in docs/, and the README links to the same help pages.

docs.title: A game is unmatched
docs.text: Sync catalogs, then refresh from catalog. If it is still unmatched, use manual search, paste the source page URL, or mark it as not in catalog.

docs.title: A match opens the wrong site
docs.text: Edit the match and choose the correct source entry. Adult Game Manager stores source-aware matches, so the selected source controls the page URL.

docs.title: Catalog results look old
docs.text: Run catalog sync again. If sync fails, save diagnostics and check the log for catalog URL or network errors.

docs.title: Update check still shows an old version
docs.text: Check loaded config summaries in diagnostics logs. An old private config may override version feeds. AGM should report how many version feeds are active without printing private tokens.

docs.title: Diagnostics upload menu is missing
docs.text: Upload actions only appear when diagnostics upload is configured. You can still use local diagnostics save and copy diagnostics summary.

docs.title: Does the app download games?
docs.text: No. It tracks local apps/games, searches catalogs, opens source pages, and helps install local files you choose.

docs.title: Getting started
docs.text: Install Adult Game Manager from GitHub Releases, open it, and let it scan your installed APKs and optional JoiPlay games.

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
1. Download the latest APK from GitHub Releases.
  2. Open the APK on your Android device.
  3. Approve Android's install-from-browser or install-from-files prompt if needed.
  4. Open Adult Game Manager.

docs.title: First run
docs.text: On first launch, the app scans installed apps and loads the default catalog/update configuration. If F95 Updater is installed on the same device, Adult Game Manager may offer to import compatible local mapping data from it.

docs.title: First things to try
docs.text:
1. Tap Refresh from catalog to update matches.
  2. Use the search box to filter your installed games.
  3. Open the Catalog tab to search all known catalog entries.
  4. Open Menu -> JoiPlay ... -> JoiPlay settings if you use JoiPlay games.
  5. Open Menu -> Help ... -> About for quick tips and links.

docs.title: If you used F95 Updater
docs.text: Adult Game Manager has a different Android package name and local storage area, so it can live beside F95 Updater during transition. Use the migration prompt or a backup import to bring over supported mappings and app state.

docs.title: What the app does not do
docs.text: Adult Game Manager does not download paid, hosted, or third-party game files for you. It helps you track, match, install local files you select, and open the relevant page.

docs.title: JoiPlay
docs.text: JoiPlay tools help track and install local Ren'Py, RPG Maker, HTML, Tyrano, and similar games.

docs.title: Where to find it
docs.text: Menu -> JoiPlay ...

docs.title: Common actions
docs.text:
• Install game in JoiPlay - select a local game file/archive and send it through the JoiPlay install flow.
  • Import JoiPlay backup - import a .joiback file so Adult Game Manager can list your JoiPlay games.
  • JoiPlay settings - configure paths and behavior used by JoiPlay helpers.
  • Probably unused JoiPlay folders - review folders that do not appear in a fresh backup.

Archive install/upgrade actions create safety prompts and backups where possible. Review prompts before replacing a game folder.

docs.title: Main screen
docs.text: The main screen shows installed APKs and imported JoiPlay games with their current match/update state.

docs.title: Top actions
docs.text:
Open the main menu from the top-right action button.

Common actions include:

  • Refresh from catalog - checks catalog matches and update status.
  • Check for app update - checks whether a newer Adult Game Manager APK is available.
  • JoiPlay ... - opens JoiPlay install/import/settings tools.
  • Backup & config ... - opens backup, restore, and config tools.
  • Help ... - opens documentation, About, support, and diagnostics.

docs.title: Filtering
docs.text: Use the search field and filter chips to narrow the list. Catalog tag filters use tag: terms, such as tag:renpy or multiple tags in the same query.

docs.title: Status and update state
docs.text: Each row represents a local APK or imported JoiPlay game. Matched games can show a known catalog version, installed version, source page link, and update state. Unmatched games can be matched manually, reviewed later, or marked as not in the catalog.

docs.title: Multi-select
docs.text: Long-press or use the selection controls when available to apply supported actions to multiple rows. Use this carefully for actions that can change mapping or visibility state.

docs.title: Landscape dialogs
docs.text:
AGM adapts larger workflow dialogs for landscape screens. When there is a natural split, the app shows controls and lists side-by-side instead of stacking everything vertically.

Examples include:

  • Ren'Py and RPGM save editors.
  • Save compare dialogs.
  • File and folder pickers.
  • Save-location reports and association pickers.
  • JoiPlay settings and extracted-file browsers.
  • Catalog entry details with cover art beside metadata.

docs.title: Permissions
docs.text: Adult Game Manager asks Android for permissions only when a feature needs access to local apps, files, or app installation.

docs.title: Installed apps
docs.text: The app needs to see installed applications so it can list APK games and compare them with catalog entries. Android may show this as an installed-apps visibility permission depending on the device and Android version.

docs.title: Files and folders
docs.text:
File access is used for local APKs, JoiPlay archives, .joiback backups, exported backups, saved diagnostics, screenshots, and optional drop-in config files.

Use the Android picker when possible. If a device requires broader storage access for a workflow, grant it only if you are comfortable with that access.

docs.title: Installing APKs
docs.text: If you use Install APK, Android may ask you to allow installs from the browser, file manager, or Adult Game Manager. This is an Android system prompt. Adult Game Manager does not bypass Android's installer.

docs.title: Diagnostics upload
docs.text: Diagnostics upload is not automatic. Upload actions only appear when upload settings are configured, and uploads happen only after you tap the upload action.

docs.title: Self-update
docs.text: Self-update checks configured version feeds for a newer Adult Game Manager APK.

docs.title: Where to find it
docs.text: Main menu -> Check for app update.

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: The app reads configured version.json feeds, compares the latest version/code with the installed app, and opens the APK download/install flow when an update is available.

docs.title: Current public feed
docs.text: Public releases are hosted from the AdvancedAppCreator/adult-game-manager-releases GitHub release assets.

docs.title: If it says no version configured
docs.text: Check whether an old or private app_config.json overrides update feed URLs. Recent AGM versions fall back to default feeds when blank legacy values are present.

docs.title: Support
docs.text: Use GitHub Issues for bugs and feature requests.

docs.title: Links
docs.text:
• GitHub Issues
  • Support thread
  • Latest APK

docs.title: What to include
docs.text:
1. Adult Game Manager version.
  2. Android version and device model.
  3. What you tapped.
  4. What happened instead.
  5. Saved or uploaded diagnostics when available.

docs.title: Trust and verification
docs.text: Adult Game Manager is designed to be local-first and verifiable.

docs.title: Current release
docs.text:
Item Value Version v1.0.62 APK AdultGameManager-1.0.62-release.apk APK SHA-256 43934AEE1D32666EF24B541926A458C3FF60A71ED5A8C3C4BBC2C0E1D98406A4 Source ZIP AdultGameManager-1.0.62-source.zip Source ZIP SHA-256 511D48B59B8F8753850BBDECE8250F1286B3EB00840147793297DD690DE83F5E Source repository AdvancedAppCreator/adult-game-manager Version metadata version.json

VirusTotal hash lookup for the APK, if/when submitted:

https://www.virustotal.com/gui/file/43934AEE1D32666EF24B541926A458C3FF60A71ED5A8C3C4BBC2C0E1D98406A4

docs.title: Source and license
docs.text:
The public source repository and source ZIP include:

  • Apache-2.0 project license,
  • privacy and permissions notes,
  • third-party notices,
  • release checksums,
  • a GitHub Actions workflow for build/test/lint transparency.

Third-party components keep their original licenses. In particular, bundled UnRAR source has its own license and restrictions.

docs.title: Privacy basics
docs.text:
• No F95 login is required.
  • No analytics SDK is included.
  • No automatic game downloader is included.
  • Catalog/update metadata is downloaded from public release URLs.
  • Logs stay local unless the user explicitly saves/uploads them.
  • Public defaults do not include private upload endpoints.

docs.title: Build transparency
docs.text:
The source ZIP can be inspected or built locally with JDK 17 and Android SDK API 34:

.\gradlew.bat testDebugUnitTest lintDebug assembleRelease

Official release APKs are signed separately. Signing files are not part of the source distribution.

docs.title: Trust and verification
docs.text: Adult Game Manager is designed to be local-first and verifiable.

docs.text:

docs.title: Source and license
docs.text:
The public source repository and source ZIP include:

  • Apache-2.0 project license,
  • privacy and permissions notes,
  • third-party notices,
  • release checksums,
  • a GitHub Actions workflow for build/test/lint transparency.

Third-party components keep their original licenses. In particular, bundled UnRAR source has its own license and restrictions.

docs.title: Privacy basics
docs.text:
• No F95 login is required.
  • No analytics SDK is included.
  • No automatic game downloader is included.
  • Catalog/update metadata is downloaded from public release URLs.
  • Logs stay local unless the user explicitly saves/uploads them.
  • Public defaults do not include private upload endpoints.

docs.title: Build transparency
docs.text:
The source ZIP can be inspected or built locally with JDK 17 and Android SDK API 34:

.\gradlew.bat testDebugUnitTest lintDebug assembleRelease

Official release APKs are signed separately. Signing files are not part of the source distribution.

docs.title: App config
docs.text: app_config.json is an advanced override file for release, catalog, support, donation, and optional diagnostics-upload settings.

docs.title: Where to put it
docs.text: Adult Game Manager looks for drop-in config files in common Documents and Download locations using the app's Adult Game Manager folder names.

docs.title: What it can override
docs.text:
• Catalog and source index URLs.
  • Version/update feed URLs.
  • Fallback APK URL.
  • Help, issues, support, and donation links.
  • Optional diagnostics upload settings.
  • Optional feature flags.

docs.title: Safety
docs.text: The app logs only a safe config summary. It must not print upload authorization tokens or private query strings in diagnostics logs.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: If a config is loaded, logs should include a private config summary showing counts and booleans, not secrets. Blank legacy fields should fall back to AGM defaults where supported.

docs.title: Auto-backups
docs.text: Auto-backups protect app state before risky operations when the feature is available for that workflow.

docs.title: What they are
docs.text: An auto-backup is a local backup created by Adult Game Manager before an operation that may change mappings, imports, or JoiPlay-related state.

docs.title: Where they are saved
docs.text: Auto-backups are saved to local app/document storage according to Android file-access rules and app configuration.

docs.title: Recommendation
docs.text: Use manual export before major changes even if auto-backups are enabled.

docs.title: Export and import backup
docs.text: Backups preserve Adult Game Manager state such as mappings, hidden/not-in-catalog flags, and personal tracking data included by the app.

docs.title: Where to find it
docs.text: Menu -> Backup & config ... -> Export backup or Import backup.

docs.title: Export before
docs.text:
• Moving to a new device.
  • Testing prerelease builds.
  • Large matching or cleanup sessions.
  • Changing advanced config.

docs.title: Import
docs.text: Choose a backup file and review the restored state. Imports affect app data, not installed APKs or game files.

docs.title: Auto-hide non-games
docs.text: Auto-hide helps reduce clutter from installed apps that are clearly not games.

docs.title: Where to find it
docs.text: Catalog or main-menu cleanup tools when available.

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: Adult Game Manager can hide known non-game apps or rows you marked as not in catalog. Hidden rows are not deleted from your device.

docs.title: Undo
docs.text: Use filters or row actions to show hidden/not-in-catalog entries again, then clear the hidden or not-in-catalog state.

docs.title: Browse and filter
docs.text: Catalog filters help you find games across all configured source catalogs.

docs.title: Where to find it
docs.text: Open the Catalog tab.

docs.title: Common filters
docs.text: Filter Use Source Limit results to one catalog source. Platform Show Android, Windows, Linux, Mac, or other platform metadata when available. Installed state Show installed, matched, or not-installed entries. Tags Search by one or more catalog tags. Status / engine / rating Narrow broad result sets. Sort Change result order.

docs.title: Tips
docs.text: Use source and platform chips first, then add tags or title search to narrow large catalogs.

docs.title: Refresh installed games
docs.text: Refresh from catalog updates installed-game rows using the current catalog data.

docs.title: Where to find it
docs.text: Main menu -> Refresh from catalog.

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: Adult Game Manager compares local games against cached catalog entries, updates match candidates, refreshes version/update state, and keeps source-specific links attached to matched games.

docs.title: Before refreshing
docs.text: Sync catalogs first if you have not refreshed them recently. Refreshing with stale catalog data can leave update state unchanged.

docs.title: If a row stays unmatched
docs.text: Use Manual search, Paste a page URL, or Review unmapped games.

docs.title: Review unmapped games
docs.text: Review unmapped games walks through local rows that are not yet linked to a catalog entry.

docs.title: Where to find it
docs.text: Catalog tools -> Review unmapped games.

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: The review flow shows likely matches and lets you accept a match, search manually, paste a page URL, skip, or mark the row as not in the catalog.

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
• Sync catalogs before starting a review pass.
  • Use source names and URLs to avoid accepting a same-title match from the wrong source.
  • Skip uncertain rows rather than forcing a weak match.

docs.title: Sources and sync
docs.text: Catalog sync downloads the configured source index and source catalog files so Adult Game Manager can search and match games locally.

docs.title: Where to find it
docs.text: Use Catalog tools or refresh actions from the main menu.

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: The app fetches a catalog index, then loads enabled source catalogs such as F95Zone and AdultGameWorld. Each entry keeps source ID, title, source URL, version text, tags, platforms, rating, and other source-specific metadata.

docs.title: When to sync
docs.text:
• After installing a new AGM version.
  • Before reviewing unmapped games.
  • If catalog search results look stale.
  • After changing catalog URLs in app_config.json.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: If sync fails, check network connectivity, then use Diagnostics and logs to save a local log.

docs.title: Diagnostics and logs
docs.text: Diagnostics tools help troubleshoot Adult Game Manager behavior without guessing.

docs.title: Where to find it
docs.text: Menu -> Help ....

docs.title: Always available
docs.text:
• Copy diagnostics summary opens a summary you can copy into an issue.
  • Save local diagnostics writes logs and queued crash reports to your Documents folder.

docs.title: Optional upload actions
docs.text:
When diagnostics upload is enabled in app_config.json, more actions may appear:

  • Upload crash logs uploads queued crash reports to the configured upload target.
  • Upload app logs + screenshots uploads current app logs plus captured screenshots.
  • Capture launch screenshots captures launch/onboarding screens for docs or support.
  • Capture walkthrough screenshots captures the main help-site walkthrough screens.
  • Verbose logs toggles extra logging for the current run.

docs.title: Privacy
docs.text:
Diagnostics upload is explicit. Adult Game Manager does not upload logs or screenshots automatically, and upload actions only appear when upload settings are configured.

Before sharing diagnostics publicly, review them for private paths, filenames, or other details you do not want to publish.

docs.title: What to look for in logs
docs.text:
• App version and Android/device details.
  • Catalog sync start/failure messages.
  • Update-feed URL counts and selected version.
  • Safe private config summary, such as how many version feeds are configured.
  • Crash stack traces or failed operation messages.

docs.title: Install APK
docs.text: Install APK opens a local APK file you choose and hands it to Android's normal package installer.

docs.title: Where to find it
docs.text: Main menu or install tools -> Install APK.

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: Adult Game Manager lets you pick an APK from local storage, then starts Android's install flow. Android decides whether the APK can be installed and may ask you to allow installs from that source.

docs.title: What it does not do
docs.text: The app does not download APKs for you, bypass Android security prompts, or silently install packages.

docs.title: Delete a JoiPlay game
docs.text: Delete actions remove a local JoiPlay game folder only after you confirm the operation.

docs.title: Where to find it
docs.text: Open a JoiPlay game row and use the delete/remove action when available.

docs.title: Safety
docs.text: Deletion affects local files. Export a JoiPlay backup or copy the folder elsewhere before deleting if you may want to restore it.

docs.title: If deletion fails
docs.text: Android storage restrictions or file locks can prevent deletion. Close JoiPlay, retry, or remove the folder with your file manager.

docs.title: Import .joiback backup
docs.text: Importing a .joiback file lets Adult Game Manager list and track JoiPlay games.

docs.title: Where to find it
docs.text: Menu -> JoiPlay ... -> Import JoiPlay backup.

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
1. Export a .joiback backup from JoiPlay.
  2. In Adult Game Manager, choose Import JoiPlay backup.
  3. Select the backup file.
  4. Review imported games on the main screen.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: The import reads local metadata from the backup. It does not download game files.

docs.title: Install game in JoiPlay
docs.text: Install game in JoiPlay helps send a local archive or folder through the JoiPlay install/import flow.

docs.title: Where to find it
docs.text: Menu -> JoiPlay ... -> Install game in JoiPlay.

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: Choose a local game archive or file. Adult Game Manager prepares the handoff and uses configured JoiPlay settings to help install or update the game.

docs.title: Safety
docs.text: Read prompts before replacing an existing game folder. When an operation may overwrite data, keep a backup of the original game folder or JoiPlay backup.

docs.title: JoiPlay settings
docs.text: JoiPlay settings configure how Adult Game Manager finds and manages local JoiPlay games.

docs.title: Where to find it
docs.text: Menu -> JoiPlay ... -> JoiPlay settings.

docs.title: Common settings
docs.text:
• JoiPlay game folder locations.
  • Import behavior for .joiback backups.
  • Install/update behavior for local archives.
  • Safety options used before replacing folders.

On landscape screens, source and destination folder settings are shown side-by-side so it is easier to compare the selected folders.

docs.title: Tips
docs.text: If imported games do not appear, confirm the folder location and import a fresh .joiback backup from JoiPlay.

docs.title: Probably unused folders
docs.text: Probably unused folders helps find JoiPlay folders that do not appear in a fresh JoiPlay backup.

docs.title: Where to find it
docs.text: Menu -> JoiPlay ... -> Probably unused JoiPlay folders.

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: Adult Game Manager compares known imported JoiPlay games with folders in configured locations and highlights folders that may no longer be referenced.

docs.title: Be careful
docs.text: This is a cleanup aid, not proof that a folder is safe to delete. Check folder contents before removing anything.

docs.title: Auto-match
docs.text: Auto-match links installed games to catalog entries using known package names, titles, and normalized name matching.

docs.title: Where to find it
docs.text: Use Refresh from catalog from the main menu, or sync the catalog and then refresh installed games.

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: Adult Game Manager compares local APK and JoiPlay names with the current source catalogs. When a confident match is found, the row can show the source page, known catalog version, and update state.

docs.title: Source-aware matching
docs.text: Matches preserve their source. A game matched to AdultGameWorld opens the AdultGameWorld page; a game matched to F95Zone opens the F95Zone page.

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
• Sync catalogs before judging match quality.
  • Use manual search for alternate titles, abbreviations, or games with very generic names.
  • Mark non-game apps or unrelated entries as not in the catalog so they stop distracting you.

docs.title: Change a wrong match
docs.text: If a local game is linked to the wrong catalog entry, edit the match instead of deleting the game.

docs.title: Where to find it
docs.text: Open the game row and choose the match/edit action.

docs.title: What to do
docs.text:
1. Clear the incorrect match if needed.
  2. Search for the correct title or paste the correct page URL.
  3. Confirm the replacement match.
  4. Refresh from catalog to update the displayed version and source link.

docs.title: Why it matters
docs.text: Wrong matches can show the wrong update state and open the wrong page. Source-aware matching keeps the selected source and page URL attached to the local game.

docs.title: Manual search
docs.text: Manual search lets you choose the correct catalog entry when auto-match is missing or wrong.

docs.title: Where to find it
docs.text: Open an installed game row and choose the match/edit action, or use the review-unmapped flow from the Catalog tools.

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: Search by title, compare source names and page URLs, then apply the best match. The app stores the selected source entry for that local game.

docs.title: Good search terms
docs.text:
• Use the shortest distinctive part of the game title.
  • Remove version numbers, platform tags, and punctuation.
  • Try alternate spellings if the catalog source uses a different title.

docs.title: If no result is correct
docs.text: Use Paste a page URL if you know the exact source page, or mark it as not in catalog if the local app should be ignored.

docs.title: Mark not in catalog
docs.text: Marking a row as not in catalog tells Adult Game Manager not to keep suggesting matches for that local app or game.

docs.title: Where to find it
docs.text: Open the match/edit action for a row, or use the review-unmapped workflow.

docs.title: When to use it
docs.text:
• The installed app is not an adult game.
  • The game is private, modified, or not listed in any configured source.
  • Auto-match repeatedly suggests unrelated entries.

docs.title: Undo
docs.text: Open the row's match/edit action again and clear the not-in-catalog state if you want it to be considered for matching later.

docs.title: Paste a page URL
docs.text: Paste URL matching links a local game to a known source page when search is not enough.

docs.title: Where to find it
docs.text: Open the manual match/edit dialog for a game and use the URL field when available.

docs.title: What it accepts
docs.text: Use a normal source page URL, such as an F95Zone thread URL or another configured source URL. Adult Game Manager stores the source-aware link so the row can open the right page later.

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
• Paste the canonical page, not a temporary search-result URL.
  • If the app cannot parse the URL, use manual search or refresh the source catalog first.

docs.text:

Current filenames:
